destinations.evidence: DKMS validation rejects zero owner after base executor/TTL validation.
The codec decodes owner as an explicit DKMS field before validation checks it.
destinations.snippet:
owner: address_token(&tokens[5], "owner")?,
key_index: u64_token(&tokens[6], "key_index")?,
key_format: u8_token(&tokens[7], "key_format")?,

equivalence_argument: The validation path decodes owner from the DKMS ABI and fails closed with OwnerZeroAddress while preserving existing executor and TTL checks.
notes: This maps directly to the current validation.rs DKMS branch.
rationale: The validation diff updates the DKMS validation comment and adds an explicit Address::ZERO owner check returning InvalidRequest before the existing TTL checks. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: DKMS key payload validation must reject requests whose owner address is zero, in addition to rejecting zero executor address, zero TTL, and TTL above the configured maximum.
suggested_falsification_tests: Build DKMS request calldata with owner set to zero and otherwise valid fields, assert validate_dkms_key_payload returns InvalidRequest; change owner to nonzero and assert the request passes the owner check while TTL/executor validation still behaves as before.
summary: DKMS payload validation rejects zero owner.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Build DKMS request calldata with owner set to zero and otherwise valid fields, assert validate_dkms_key_payload returns InvalidRequest; change owner to nonzero and assert the request passes the owner check while TTL/executor validation still behaves as before.
title: DKMS payload requires owner
